_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-06 05:56:56Z)**_

The grid creates `STRIP_STEPS` columns but only renders visible content in column index 4 (the 5th cell). The other cells are empty `<div className="h-16">` elements — they exist solely to position the colored button at a specific grid column.

This is a spacing hack. The button could just be placed directly with CSS instead of using empty grid cells as spacers. A simpler approach:

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-06 05:57:06Z)**_

It's a 9-column grid where only cell 4 has content — 8 invisible divs just to position one button. It looks like this was done to align the hue swatch with the 5th column of the palette strips rendered elsewhere in the component.

You could replace this with a single element using `grid-column: 5` or just a `margin-left` / percentage offset, which would be cleaner. Want me to simplify it?
